items.title: Railways to seek bundled approvals for bullet trains

The Alliance for American Solar Manufacturing and Trade welcomed the move. Final decisions are expected later this year.

items.title: Meta, Microsoft Look to Trim Workforces Amid Heavy AI Spending

This decision favors Sun Pharmaceutical Industries in a decade-long trademark dispute. The court found the names deceptively similar to Sun Pharma's established marks. The ruling prevents confusion among consumers in the pharmaceutical market.

items.summary: Classic Legends is planning to expand its production capacity. This includes considering a new factory as its current plant will soon reach its limit. Marine and plantation exports are showing strong momentum. Pulses, fresh fruits, and vegetable oils are key contributors. Processed food segments are also expanding, highlighting value addition's role in boosting exports.

items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee highlighted that the West Asia conflict poses significant downside risks to India's growth and upside risks to inflation. Public infrastructure projects could be deferred to manage expenditure.

items.title: Devina Mehra: Investors beware—Part-truths could be more misleading than complete lies
items.summary: Part-truths create the sort of over-confidence that often leads people to delude themselves. Tales about compounding, blue chips or market winners that don’t offer the full picture can let investors down worse than outright falsehoods. Examples abound.

items.title: The RBI governor’s Princeton speech is spot on: India isn’t ready yet for full capital account liberalization

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | Will Tim Cook’s successor at Apple turn to chips and gizmos in the face of AI?

items.title: India, Taiwan get time till October to settle ICT tariff dispute
items.summary: India and Taiwan have requested the WTO's dispute settlement body to postpone a ruling on India's import duties on ICT products until October 27. Both nations are actively engaged in mutual discussions to resolve the dispute. This marks the ninth such delay, as previous requests were also granted.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh mango pulp industry seeks central support
items.summary: Mango processors in Andhra Pradesh face export challenges. A significant quantity of mango pulp is stuck due to shipping lane disruptions. Processors are requesting government support. They seek equity capital and credit guarantees. Moratoriums on loan payments are also requested. Inclusion in export facilitation schemes is a priority.

items.summary: The MoU creates a structured framework for collaboration between the two countries, enabling continuous dialogue on key MSME issues and encouraging exploration of mutually beneficial opportunities, the ministry said in a statement.

items.summary: In an effort to invigorate the co-operative banking landscape, the Reserve Bank of India is contemplating a significant reduction in the minimum capital requirement for urban co-operative bank licenses. The suggested cap is set to decrease from ₹300 crore to a more accessible ₹200 crore.

items.title: WTO accepts request of India, Taipei to defer ICT import duty dispute ruling until Oct 2026
items.summary: India and Taiwan have secured a delay in a WTO ruling on India's import duties for certain IT products. The decision is now postponed until October 2026. Both nations are actively engaged in discussions to find a mutual resolution to this trade dispute. This marks the ninth such deferral requested by the parties involved.

Despite a decline in US shipments, gains in alternative markets offset losses. Export volumes hit 19.32 lakh tonnes, with key ports handling most shipments.
